votes.evidence: at the **commit level**, `feat/pairwise-metric` is carrying 5 commits ahead of `main`, 3 of which ... are not new work at all — they're the exact same weighted-scorer changes already shipped in `main` under commit `1971f08` ... because the branch was built on top of `feat/weighted-scorer` before that work was squash-merged, and `feat/pairwise-metric` was never rebased afterward

disputes.rule: two-of-three majority: the two primary judges' marks plus the adjudicator's mark; because the primaries split one-to-one on a disputed mark, the majority equals the adjudicator's mark, and the mark stays in every denominator

adjudicator_mark.evidence: **Rebase `feat/pairwise-metric` onto `main`, replaying only the commits unique to it, using `feat/weighted-scorer` as the "old base" marker** — i.e. the equivalent of `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric`. This drops the 3 duplicate W1-W3 commits (their content is already in `main` via `1971f08`) and replays only `7b2e100` (X1) and `1bfecb6` (X2) on top of `main`'s tip. I verified this path is conflict-free: `git merge-tree ac9d505 main feat/pairwise-metric` (using the old shared base) produces a clean merge whose resulting `scoring/metrics.py` and `prompts/grader.txt` exactly match `feat/pairwise-metric`'s current content — confirming X1/X2 apply cleanly on top of `main` once the duplicate commits are removed.
